steps.title: Compare the two examples
steps.content: Cuba shows a strong continuing legacy of the Spanish Empire because Spanish is still spoken there. Sardinia is introduced as a former colony where the tie is so weak that Spanish is seldom spoken.

steps.title: Signal the opposition
steps.content: Choice A marks frequency, not contrast. Choice C suggests simultaneity rather than opposition. Choice D adds a similar point, but Sardinia is the opposite kind of example from Cuba. The transition should mark contrast between cases. The answer is B.
